name: CRT Ontology (Customer Role Taxonomy)
description: Universal customer-side role taxonomy — B2B buyer roles, B2C consumer archetypes, and B2B2C intermediary roles with structured qualification criteria, industry role profiles, and purchase patterns. Maps to VP-ONT stakeholder types, RRR-ONT organisational roles, and ORG-CONTEXT industry/segment context.

components.relationships: 9 relationships including 3 cross-ontology bridges (CRT→VP, CRT→RRR, CRT→ORG-CONTEXT)
components.businessRules: 4 rules (3 mandatory, 1 advisory) in IF-THEN format
components.enums: channel (3 values), category (14 values), criteriaType (6), decisionStyle (5), buyerJourneyType (6), budgetAuthority (3), typicalEngagement (4), priceElasticity (3), purchaseFrequency (4)

notes: v1.0.0: Initial release. B2B/B2C/B2B2C customer-side role archetypes with structured qualification criteria and industry profiles. VHF Nutrition (6 B2C roles) and Antiques (19 roles: 10 B2B + 7 B2C + 2 B2B2C) as first instance data sets. Reinforces VP↔RRR alignment convention with role-specific problem/solution/benefit modelling.
